zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Anne de Darcy, Countess of Badsworth and Ormond於1332年4月13日出生在愛爾蘭迪 本 米亞斯時，她的父親Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I48歲，母親Joan de Burgh42歲。她於1346年5月15日與James Boteler 2nd Earl of Ormond在愛爾蘭庫迪 本 馮 克羅斯 本 迪拉利結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她於1389年住在愛爾蘭萊音斯特。她去世於1390年3月24日在愛爾蘭庫迪 多聶加爾基爾堅尼享年57歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Elizabeth Anne de Darcy, Countess of Badsworth and Ormond于1332年4月13日出生在爱尔兰迪 本 米亞斯时，她的父亲Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I48岁，母亲Joan de Burgh42岁。她于1346年5月15日与James Boteler 2nd Earl of Ormond在爱尔兰庫迪 本 馮 克羅斯 本 迪拉利结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她于1389年住在爱尔兰萊音斯特。她去世于1390年3月24日在爱尔兰庫迪 多聶加爾基爾堅尼享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Darcy,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Eamon, Brendan, Eamonn, Maeve, Malachy, Niamh, Seamus.  French Gisele, Julien, Normand, Alain, Armand, Cecile, Clemence, Dominique, Emile, Gilles, Jean-Paul, Laure.
English (Lancashire) and Irish: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Arcy in Manche, France, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’.

Irish: in Ireland, this is generally the Norman name (see 1 above), established in Meath by Sir William D'Arcy in the 1360s, but it was also adopted for Ó Dorchaidhe ‘descendant of the dark one’, from dorcha ‘dark, gloomy’, a Connacht name that fell together with the Norman surname, which was introduced by Sir William D'Arcy and Sir John D'Arcy, Chief Justiciar of Ireland in the 14th century.

variants.name: Darsey
meanings: Irish: variant of   Darcy   2.
Scottish: from Dairsie in Fife.

variants.name: Darcey
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and Irish (Tipperary): variant of   Darcy  .

nameMeanings.name: Countess
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire, of Norman origin): from Middle English contas(e), Old French contesse ‘countess’, applied as a nickname for a proud, haughty woman or for a foppish man, or as an occupational name for a servant of a countess.

variants.name: Countiss
meanings: English: variant of   Countess  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Badsworth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Bettesworth, a habitational name from Betchworth in Surrey, from the Old English personal name Becci + worth ‘enclosure’.

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Jasvir, Dalbir, Jagdish.  German Alois, Ewald, Gerda.
Indian (Punjab): Sikh name, probably from Punjabi məṇḍ ‘skill’. It is based on the name of a Jat clan.
German: variant of   Mandt  .

variants.name: Mandt
meanings: German and Norwegian (of Danish origin): from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal names beginning with Mant-, e.g. Mantwic, formed with Old High German mandjan ‘rejoice’ as the first element.

variants.name: Mander
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Balwinder, Bikram.
English: occupational name from Middle English mander, maunder ‘basket-maker’, a derivative of Middle English mande, maunde ‘basket’. Compare Mand .
German: habitational name from any of the places called Mandern, in Hesse and the Rhineland.

nameMeanings.name: Ormond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Waterford): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Ruaidh ‘descendant of Ruadh’ (a byname meaning ‘red’; see Roe ), due to similarity in sound with Urmhumha (now Urmhumhain), the Irish form of the placename Ormond, an ancient region of East Munster. The surname Ormond was also adopted by the illegitimate sons of John Butler, 6th Earl of Ormond (died 1476). The most famous and powerful of them was Sir James Ormond (died 1497), Lord High Treasurer of Ireland from 1492 to 1494.
English: variant of   Almond   or   Orman  .

variants.name: Ormonde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Adelino, Ana, Avelino, Bernardo, Blanca, Eugenio, Filipe, Helio, Mario, Serafina.
Irish (Offaly, Wexford): variant of   Ormond  . This surname is also found in Portugal, having been taken there by an immigrant Irish family.

variants.name: Ormand
meanings: English and Scottish (Perthshire): variant of Irish   Ormond  .

variants.name: Roe
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English ro(o) ‘roe deer’ (Old English rā). This is the midland and southern form, also found in South Yorkshire and Lancashire. In much of northern England, however, rā remained unrounded and survives as Ray 3.
English: post-medieval variant of Wroe (see Wray ). Wroe may also have been used as a spelling of Roe .
English: post-medieval variant of   Row   (or vice versa); see   Rowe  .

variants.name: Orman
meanings: Irish, Scottish, and English: variant of   Ormond  .
English (southern): late variant of Awmon(d), itself a variant of Almond , Hammon , or Hammond . The name may also have been confused with Herman or Harman .
English: variant of   Horman  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Anne de Darcy, Countess of Badsworth and Ormond was born on 13 April 1332, in Diocese of Meath, Ireland, her father, Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I, was 48 and her mother, Joan de Burgh, was 42. She married James Boteler 2nd Earl of Ormond on 15 May 1346, in County of the Cross of Tipperary, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She lived in Leinster, Ireland in 1389. She died on 24 March 1390, in Kilkenny, County Donegal, Ireland, at the age of 57.

fr.County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irelan: County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irlande
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Elizabeth Anne de Darcy, Countess of Badsworth and Ormond est née le 13 avril 1332, à Diocese of Meath, Irlande, son père, Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Joan de Burgh, avait 42 ans. Elle a épousé James Boteler 2nd Earl of Ormond le 15 mai 1346, à County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Elle a vécu à Leinster, Irlande en 1389. Elle est décédée le 24 mars 1390, à Kilkenny, County Donegal, Irlande, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irelan: County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irland
de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Elizabeth Anne de Darcy, Countess of Badsworth and Ormond am 13. April 1332 in Diocese of Meath, Irland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I 48 und ihre Mutter Joan de Burgh 42. Sie hat am 15. Mai 1346 in County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irland James Boteler 2nd Earl of Ormond geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. 1389 hat sie in Leinster, Irland gelebt. Sie starb am 24. März 1390 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Kilkenny, County Donegal, Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Anne de Darcy, Countess of Badsworth and Ormond è nata il il 13 aprile 1332 nel Diocese of Meath, Irlanda, sua padre, Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I, aveva 48 anni e sua madre, Joan de Burgh, aveva 42 anni. Lei sposato James Boteler 2nd Earl of Ormond il 15 maggio 1346, nel County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Vissuto a Leinster, Irlanda nel 1389. Morì il 24 marzo 1390, nel Kilkenny, County Donegal, Irlanda, all'età di 57 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Anne de Darcy, Countess of Badsworth and Ormondが1332年4月13日にアイルランドディオセゼ オフ メアスで生まれた時，父Lord John Le Neveu Darcy Iは48歳，母Joan de Burghは42歳であった。1346年5月15日にアイルランドコウントイ オフ ス クロス オフ ティップエラリーでJames Boteler 2nd Earl of Ormondと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1389年にはアイルランドレインスターに住んでいた。彼女は1390年3月24日にアイルランドコウントイ ドンエガルキルケニーで57歳で死去した。

ko.County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irelan: 아일랜드 카운티 프 테 크로스 프 티페라리
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Anne de Darcy, Countess of Badsworth and Ormond(이)가 1332년 4월 13일 아일랜드 디오스스 프 미스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I(은)는 48세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan de Burgh(은)는 42세였다. 그녀는 1346년 5월 15일 아일랜드 카운티 프 테 크로스 프 티페라리에서 James Boteler 2nd Earl of Ormond(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1389년도에 아일랜드 레인스터에서 살았다. 그녀는 1390년 3월 24일 아일랜드 카운티 도네걸 킬켄이에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.13 April 1332–24 March 1390: 13 de abril de 1332–24 de março de 1390
pt.County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irelan: County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irlanda
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Anne de Darcy, Countess of Badsworth and Ormond nasceu em 13 de abril de 1332, em Diocese of Meath, Irlanda, seu pai, Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Burgh, tinha 42 anos. Ela casou-se com James Boteler 2nd Earl of Ormond em 15 de maio de 1346, em County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela viveu em Leinster, Irlanda em 1389. Ela faleceu em 24 de março de 1390, em Kilkenny, County Donegal, Irlanda, com 57 anos.

ro.County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irelan: County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irlanda

ru.County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irelan: коунти оф т кросс оф типпирэри, Ирландия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth Anne de Darcy, Countess of Badsworth and Ormond родилась 13 апреля 1332 г. в местности диосес оф мит, Ирландия, её отцу по имени Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Burgh, 42. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени James Boteler 2nd Earl of Ormond 15 мая 1346 г. в местности коунти оф т кросс оф типпирэри, Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она жила в местности линстир, Ирландия в 1389 году. Она умерла 24 марта 1390 г. в местности килкенни, коунти доунегаль, Ирландия в возрасте 57 лет.

es.13 April 1332–24 March 1390: 13 de abril de 1332–24 de marzo de 1390
es.County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irelan: County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irlanda
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Elizabeth Anne de Darcy, Countess of Badsworth and Ormond nació el 13 de abril de 1332, en Diocese of Meath, Irlanda, su papá, Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Joan de Burgh, tenía 42 años. Se casó con James Boteler 2nd Earl of Ormond el 15 de mayo de 1346, en County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Vivió en Leinster, Irlanda en 1389. Murió el 24 de marzo de 1390, en Kilkenny, County Donegal, Irlanda, a la edad de 57 años.
